name: Anthralin
description: Anthralin is also referred to as Dithranol. First synthesized in 1916 [A27276], anthralin is a derivative of chrysarobin which is contained from the Goa powder. It is an active ingredient in topical formulations for the treatment of psoriasis. The chemical structure of anthralin confers a dual solubility characteristic, allowing the compound to penetrate through the epidermis [A27276]. Although the mechanism of action of anthralin is not fully elucidated, the drug have demonstrated anti-proliferative and anti-inflammatory effects on psoriatic and normal skin. Therapeutic drawbacks to the use of anthralin include irritation and discoloration of the skin [A27277].
